description: Three-way performance comparison: will Bitcoin outperform both Gold and the S&P 500 in 2026? Currently second-favorite at 30% behind Gold (54%). Each asset uses a different data source and methodology, creating resolution complexity.
question: Will Bitcoin have the best performance in 2026 (vs Gold and S&P 500)?

resolution_logic.summary: Resolves Yes if Bitcoin's percentage price change in 2026 exceeds both Gold's and S&P 500's. Each asset uses a specific data source and methodology: Bitcoin uses Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle close prices, Gold uses MarketWatch GC00 continuous contract close, S&P 500 uses Yahoo Finance ^SPX close.

triggers.description: Bitcoin YTD return exceeds both Gold and S&P 500 YTD returns
triggers.metric: BTC/USDT % change (Binance 1m candle close, Jan 1 12:00 AM ET to Dec 31 11:59 PM ET)
triggers.threshold: Greater than both Gold (MarketWatch GC00) and S&P 500 (Yahoo Finance ^SPX)

exclusions: Total return measures — only closing prices, no dividends or yield
Intraday prices — only official closes used
Preliminary or adjusted closing prices — must be the published close
resolution_logic.ambiguity_notes: Three different data sources creates operational risk. Binance uses 1-minute candle (very specific — midnight ET on Jan 1 and 11:59 PM ET on Dec 31). Gold and S&P use last trading day closes, which differ from calendar year-end. Alphabetical tiebreaker: Bitcoin beats Gold beats S&P if tied. Shortened trading days use published session close.

catalyst_dates.event: Q1 2026 performance comparison — first quarterly checkpoint
Q3 end — 3 months remaining, positioning accelerates

cross_platform_link.structural_notes: Kalshi does not have a three-way asset performance comparison market. Coverage gap.

related_markets.notes: Gold best performer at 54% — currently the favorite. BTC at 30% is second.
S&P 500 best performer at 20% — least favored. Implied ranking: Gold > BTC > S&P.
